name: Khalil Center
mission: Our wellness centers integrate Islamic spiritual approaches into professional psychology to help achieve optimal, holistic health by providing a wide variety of mental health services.
theoryOfChange: Integrating Islamic theological concepts (Aql, Ruh, Nafs) with the science of psychology to achieve optimal, holistic health and spiritual elevation.
scoreSummary: Khalil Foundation shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Practice-based evidence study published in 'Spirituality in Clinical Practice' (2023)
Research published in the Journal of Muslim Mental Health

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://khalilcenter.com/donate/zakat (Source: https://khalilcenter.com/donate/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

all_citations.claim: The Khalil Foundation reported total revenue of $2,505,061 and provides clinical mental health services.
The charity is zakat-eligible and follows specific asnaf categories for fund distribution.
The organization maintains a 71.2% program expense ratio and a fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised.
The organization trains professionals in Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) and tracks clinical appointments.
The foundation tracks lifetime psychotherapy sessions delivered and support groups hosted.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate score of 55/100, reflecting a strong alignment with niche community needs and efficient fundraising, balanced against limited financial reserves and a moderate cost per beneficiary.
areas_for_improvement: Limited liquidity with only 2.4 months of working capital available
Moderate data confidence regarding long-term impact metrics

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated as medium due to the organization's specialized focus on global health and its strong fit for Muslim donors seeking culturally congruent care [2]. The foundation reported total revenue of $2,505,061, supporting its mission to provide clinical services and community education [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates moderate impact with a 71.2% program expense ratio and a lifetime record of delivering psychotherapy sessions and support groups [3][5]. While it successfully builds capacity through TIIP training for professionals, the cost per beneficiary remains relatively high at approximately $907.87 [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Khalil Foundation integrates traditional Islamic spiritual principles with modern clinical psychology to provide comprehensive mental health services.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.01 spent to raise every $1
Strong programmatic focus with a 71.2% program expense ratio
Clear zakat eligibility and distribution policy for Muslim donors
baseline_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, provides specialized clinical mental health services, community education, and professional training rooted in Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) [1][4]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, applying funds toward eligible beneficiaries in accordance with established asnaf categories [2].

all_citations.quote: Zakat funds are used to provide TIIP-based mental health services to those in need.
Khalil Center is a psychological and spiritual wellness center. utilizing TIIP.

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate score of 55/100, reflecting a strong alignment with niche community needs and efficient fundraising, balanced against limited financial reserves and a moderate cost per beneficiary.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization currently maintains only <cite id="1">2.4 months of working capital</cite>, which is below the recommended 3-12 month range for institutional stability.
Current data indicates a <cite id="3">minimal board size of 3 members</cite>, which may limit the diversity of oversight and strategic perspectives.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB Wise Giving Alliance assessment is not currently available for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains high fundraising efficiency, which may allow for rapid capital mobilization if needed.
risk_factors: <cite id="3">Revenue CAGR of -0.7% over the last three years</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="1">low working capital</cite> and a <cite id="3">declining 3-year revenue trend</cite>.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023, impact data from 2025
confidence.notes: Strong programmatic data but governance and audit transparency could be improved.

alignment.explanation: The organization shows strong alignment with Muslim donor needs through <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and a focus on the <cite id="1">urgent cause of global health</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further strengthened by <cite id="2">clarifying specific zakat allocation policies</cite> and expanding services to more <cite id="1">underserved geographies</cite>.

credibility.explanation: The Khalil Center establishes credibility through <cite id="3">100% independent board members</cite> and a long <cite id="1">track record since 2010</cite>. However, transparency is limited by a <cite id="3">small board of only 3 members</cite>.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be enhanced by expanding the board of directors and publishing audited financial statements to increase public transparency.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="3">71.2% program expense ratio</cite> and a verified <cite id="4">capacity building delivery model</cite> that has trained hundreds of professionals.
impact.improvement: To improve impact scores, the organization should <cite id="1">build financial reserves</cite> toward a 6-month target and document a <cite id="1">clearer theory of change</cite> with explicit causal pathways.

rich_narrative.headline: The Khalil Foundation integrates traditional Islamic spiritual principles with modern clinical psychology to provide comprehensive mental health services.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize faith-based mental health solutions and want to support the professionalization of Islamic psychology through clinical training.
donor_motivations: Want to address the mental health crisis within the Muslim community
Value the integration of spiritual tradition with clinical science
Seek to fulfill zakat through health-related services for the needy
Prefer supporting established organizations with a 15-year track record
giving_considerations: Donations support a lean organization with limited cash reserves
The impact is both direct (sessions) and systemic (training others)
Revenue has been slightly declining, suggesting a high need for new funding
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with large endowment-style reserves or those looking for purely secular medical interventions.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified output tracking including <cite id="5">70,136 sessions</cite> and <cite id="4">500 professionals trained</cite>, though long-term longitudinal impact studies are not yet public.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Khalil Center uses the TIIP framework to provide culturally congruent mental health care, which increases service utilization and improves outcomes for Muslim populations.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the integration of faith does not compromise clinical standards, but rather enhances them for the target demographic.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="3">slight decline in revenue (-0.7%)</cite> and <cite id="1">low reserves</cite>, the organization has significant capacity to absorb new funding to stabilize and expand.
strategic_priorities: Increase clinical capacity in underserved regions

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: USA, Canada, UK, Pakistan, Turkey, Qatar

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="6">Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP)</cite> model which is unique among clinical peers.
peer_comparison.peer_group: Global Health and Mental Health Services

similar_organizations.differentiator: Broader medical focus beyond mental health
similar_organizations.name: IMANA

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates remarkable fiscal discipline in its development activities, spending just <cite id="1">$0.01 for every $1 raised</cite>.
A significant majority of resources are directed toward core services, with a <cite id="3">71.2% program expense ratio</cite> dedicated to clinical and educational missions.
The center maintains a <cite id="2">clear zakat policy</cite> that directs funds toward eligible <cite id="2">fuqara and masakin</cite> beneficiaries seeking mental health support.

strengths_deep_dive: The Khalil Center's primary strength lies in its unique integration of faith and science, which addresses a critical service gap in the Muslim community. By developing the <cite id="6">TIIP framework</cite>, they have moved beyond standard therapy to offer a model that resonates with the spiritual values of their clients. This model has achieved significant scale, as seen in their <cite id="5">70,136 lifetime psychotherapy sessions</cite>. Furthermore, their role as a capacity builder is evidenced by the <cite id="4">500 professionals trained</cite> in their methodology, ensuring that their impact extends far beyond their own clinical staff.
rich_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, is a specialized mental health organization that bridges the gap between modern clinical psychology and traditional Islamic spiritual principles. Since its <cite id="1">founding in 2010</cite>, the center has pioneered the <cite id="2">Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP)</cite> framework to provide culturally congruent care. The organization operates at a significant scale for a specialized provider, reporting <cite id="3">$2,272,898 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Their impact is evidenced by a high volume of direct service delivery, including <cite id="4">13,065 clinical appointments</cite> in a single year and a lifetime total of <cite id="5">70,136 psychotherapy sessions delivered</cite>. Beyond individual clinical care, the Khalil Center serves as an educational hub, having <cite id="4">trained 500 professionals in TIIP</cite> and hosted <cite id="5">52 support groups</cite> to foster community resilience. Financially, the organization demonstrates high efficiency in its development efforts, spending only <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. While the organization maintains a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 71.2%</cite>, it operates with a lean financial cushion, holding approximately <cite id="1">2.4 months of working capital</cite>. The center's geographic reach is extensive, providing services across <cite id="3">Santa Clara, Los Angeles, New York City, and Chicago</cite>, as well as international locations including <cite id="5">Canada, Pakistan, and the United Kingdom</cite>.
